root_text.ud8.6:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā magadhesu cārikaṁ caramāno mahatā bhikkhusaṅghena saddhiṁ yena pāṭaligāmo tadavasari.
root_text.ud8.6:1.4: “bhagavā kira magadhesu cārikaṁ caramāno mahatā bhikkhusaṅghena saddhiṁ pāṭaligāmaṁ anuppatto”ti.
root_text.ud8.6:1.5: Atha kho pāṭaligāmiyā upāsakā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu.
root_text.ud8.6:1.6: Ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho pāṭaligāmiyā upāsakā bhagavantaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.ud8.6:2.1: Atha kho pāṭaligāmiyā upāsakā bhagavato adhivāsanaṁ viditvā uṭṭhāyāsanā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā yenāvasathāgāraṁ tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā sabbasanthariṁ āvasathāgāraṁ santharitvā āsanāni paññāpetvā udakamaṇikaṁ patiṭṭhāpetvā telappadīpaṁ āropetvā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhaṁsu. Ekamantaṁ ṭhitā kho pāṭaligāmiyā upāsakā bhagavantaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.ud8.6:2.2: “sabbasantharisanthataṁ, bhante, āvasathāgāraṁ; āsanāni paññattāni; udakamaṇiko patiṭṭhāpito telappadīpo āropito.
root_text.ud8.6:3.1: Atha kho bhagavā nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya saddhiṁ bhikkhusaṅghena yena āvasathāgāraṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā pāde pakkhāletvā āvasathāgāraṁ pavisitvā majjhimaṁ thambhaṁ nissāya puratthābhimukho nisīdi.
root_text.ud8.6:3.2: Bhikkhusaṅghopi kho pāde pakkhāletvā āvasathāgāraṁ pavisitvā pacchimaṁ bhittiṁ nissāya puratthābhimukho nisīdi bhagavantaṁyeva purakkhatvā.
root_text.ud8.6:3.3: Pāṭaligāmiyāpi kho upāsakā pāde pakkhāletvā āvasathāgāraṁ pavisitvā puratthimaṁ bhittiṁ nissāya pacchimābhimukhā nisīdiṁsu bhagavantaṁyeva purakkhatvā.
root_text.ud8.6:3.4: Atha kho bhagavā pāṭaligāmiye upāsake āmantesi:
root_text.ud8.6:4.3: Idha, gahapatayo, dussīlo sīlavipanno pamādādhikaraṇaṁ mahatiṁ bhogajāniṁ nigacchati.
root_text.ud8.6:5.1: Puna caparaṁ, gahapatayo, dussīlassa sīlavipannassa pāpako kittisaddo abbhuggacchati.
root_text.ud8.6:6.1: Puna caparaṁ, gahapatayo, dussīlo sīlavipanno yaññadeva parisaṁ upasaṅkamati—yadi khattiyaparisaṁ, yadi brāhmaṇaparisaṁ, yadi gahapatiparisaṁ, yadi samaṇaparisaṁ—avisārado upasaṅkamati maṅkubhūto.
root_text.ud8.6:7.1: Puna caparaṁ, gahapatayo, dussīlo sīlavipanno sammūḷho kālaṁ karoti.
root_text.ud8.6:8.1: Puna caparaṁ, gahapatayo, dussīlo sīlavipanno kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ nirayaṁ upapajjati.
root_text.ud8.6:8.3: Ime kho, gahapatayo, pañca ādīnavā dussīlassa sīlavipattiyā.
root_text.ud8.6:9.3: Idha, gahapatayo, sīlavā sīlasampanno appamādādhikaraṇaṁ mahantaṁ bhogakkhandhaṁ adhigacchati.
root_text.ud8.6:10.1: Puna caparaṁ, gahapatayo, sīlavato sīlasampannassa kalyāṇo kittisaddo abbhuggacchati.
root_text.ud8.6:11.1: Puna caparaṁ, gahapatayo, sīlavā sīlasampanno yaññadeva parisaṁ upasaṅkamati—yadi khattiyaparisaṁ, yadi brāhmaṇaparisaṁ, yadi gahapatiparisaṁ, yadi samaṇaparisaṁ—visārado upasaṅkamati amaṅkubhūto.
root_text.ud8.6:12.1: Puna caparaṁ, gahapatayo, sīlavā sīlasampanno asammūḷho kālaṁ karoti.
root_text.ud8.6:13.1: Puna caparaṁ, gahapatayo, sīlavā sīlasampanno kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajjati.
root_text.ud8.6:13.3: Ime kho, gahapatayo, pañca ānisaṁsā sīlavato sīlasampadāyā”ti.
root_text.ud8.6:14.1: Atha kho bhagavā pāṭaligāmiye upāsake bahudeva rattiṁ dhammiyā kathāya sandassetvā samādapetvā samuttejetvā sampahaṁsetvā uyyojesi:
root_text.ud8.6:14.2: “abhikkantā kho, gahapatayo, ratti; yassadāni tumhe kālaṁ maññathā”ti
root_text.ud8.6:14.3: Atha kho pāṭaligāmiyā upāsakā bhagavato bhāsitaṁ abhinanditvā anumoditvā uṭṭhāyāsanā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā padakkhiṇaṁ katvā pakkamiṁsu.
root_text.ud8.6:14.4: Atha kho bhagavā acirapakkantesu pāṭaligāmiyesu upāsakesu suññāgāraṁ pāvisi.
root_text.ud8.6:15.1: Tena kho pana samayena sunidhavassakārā magadhamahāmattā pāṭaligāme nagaraṁ māpenti vajjīnaṁ paṭibāhāya.
root_text.ud8.6:15.2: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulā devatāyo sahassasahasseva pāṭaligāme vatthūni pariggaṇhanti.
root_text.ud8.6:15.3: Yasmiṁ padese mahesakkhā devatā vatthūni pariggaṇhanti mahesakkhānaṁ tattha raññaṁ rājamahāmattānaṁ cittāni namanti nivesanāni māpetuṁ.
root_text.ud8.6:15.4: Yasmiṁ padese majjhimā devatā vatthūni pariggaṇhanti majjhimānaṁ tattha raññaṁ rājamahāmattānaṁ cittāni namanti nivesanāni māpetuṁ.
root_text.ud8.6:15.5: Yasmiṁ padese nīcā devatā vatthūni pariggaṇhanti nīcānaṁ tattha raññaṁ rājamahāmattānaṁ cittāni namanti nivesanāni māpetuṁ.
root_text.ud8.6:16.1: Addasā kho bhagavā dibbena cakkhunā visuddhena atikkantamānusakena tā devatāyo sahassasahasseva pāṭaligāme vatthūni pariggaṇhantiyo.
root_text.ud8.6:16.2: Yasmiṁ padese mahesakkhā devatā vatthūni pariggaṇhanti, mahesakkhānaṁ tattha raññaṁ rājamahāmattānaṁ cittāni namanti nivesanāni māpetuṁ.
root_text.ud8.6:16.3: Yasmiṁ padese majjhimā devatā vatthūni pariggaṇhanti, majjhimānaṁ tattha raññaṁ rājamahāmattānaṁ cittāni namanti nivesanāni māpetuṁ.
root_text.ud8.6:16.4: Yasmiṁ padese nīcā devatā vatthūni pariggaṇhanti, nīcānaṁ tattha raññaṁ rājamahāmattānaṁ cittāni namanti nivesanāni māpetuṁ.
root_text.ud8.6:16.5: Atha kho bhagavā tassā rattiyā paccūsasamaye paccuṭṭhāya āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ āmantesi:
root_text.ud8.6:17.1: “Ke nu kho, ānanda, pāṭaligāme nagaraṁ māpentī”ti.
root_text.ud8.6:17.2: “Sunidhavassakārā, bhante, magadhamahāmattā pāṭaligāme nagaraṁ māpenti vajjīnaṁ paṭibāhāyā”ti.
root_text.ud8.6:17.4: evamevaṁ kho, ānanda, sunidhavassakārā magadhamahāmattā pāṭaligāme nagaraṁ māpenti vajjīnaṁ paṭibāhāya.
root_text.ud8.6:17.5: Idhāhaṁ, ānanda, addasaṁ dibbena cakkhunā visuddhena atikkantamānusakena sambahulā devatāyo sahassasahasseva pāṭaligāme vatthūni pariggaṇhantiyo.
root_text.ud8.6:17.6: Yasmiṁ padese mahesakkhā devatā vatthūni pariggaṇhanti mahesakkhānaṁ tattha raññaṁ rājamahāmattānaṁ cittāni namanti nivesanāni māpetuṁ.
root_text.ud8.6:17.7: Yasmiṁ padese majjhimā devatā vatthūni pariggaṇhanti majjhimānaṁ tattha raññaṁ rājamahāmattānaṁ cittāni namanti nivesanāni māpetuṁ.
root_text.ud8.6:17.8: Yasmiṁ padese nīcā devatā vatthūni pariggaṇhanti nīcānaṁ tattha raññaṁ rājamahāmattānaṁ cittāni namanti nivesanāni māpetuṁ.
root_text.ud8.6:17.9: Yāvatā, ānanda, ariyaṁ āyatanaṁ yāvatā vaṇippatho idaṁ agganagaraṁ bhavissati pāṭaliputtaṁ puṭabhedanaṁ.
root_text.ud8.6:17.10: Pāṭaliputtassa kho, ānanda, tayo antarāyā bhavissanti—
root_text.ud8.6:17.11: aggito vā udakato vā mithubhedato vā”ti.
root_text.ud8.6:18.1: Atha kho sunidhavassakārā magadhamahāmattā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavatā saddhiṁ sammodiṁsu.
root_text.ud8.6:18.2: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhaṁsu.
root_text.ud8.6:18.3: Ekamantaṁ ṭhitā kho sunidhavassakārā magadhamahāmattā bhagavantaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.ud8.6:18.4: “adhivāsetu no bhavaṁ gotamo ajjatanāya bhattaṁ saddhiṁ bhikkhusaṅghenā”ti.
root_text.ud8.6:19.1: Atha kho sunidhavassakārā magadhamahāmattā bhagavato adhivāsanaṁ viditvā yena sako āvasatho tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā sake āvasathe paṇītaṁ khādanīyaṁ bhojanīyaṁ paṭiyādāpetvā bhagavato kālaṁ ārocesuṁ:
root_text.ud8.6:20.1: Atha kho bhagavā pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya saddhiṁ bhikkhusaṅghena yena sunidhavassakārānaṁ magadhamahāmattānaṁ āvasatho tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā paññatte āsane nisīdi.
root_text.ud8.6:20.2: Atha kho sunidhavassakārā magadhamahāmattā buddhappamukhaṁ bhikkhusaṅghaṁ paṇītena khādanīyena bhojanīyena sahatthā santappesuṁ sampavāresuṁ.
root_text.ud8.6:21.1: Atha kho sunidhavassakārā magadhamahāmattā bhagavantaṁ bhuttāviṁ onītapattapāṇiṁ aññataraṁ nīcaṁ āsanaṁ gahetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu.
root_text.ud8.6:21.2: Ekamantaṁ nisinne kho sunidhavassakāre magadhamahāmatte bhagavā imāhi gāthāhi anumodi:
root_text.ud8.6:25.1: Atha kho bhagavā sunidhavassakārānaṁ magadhamahāmattānaṁ imāhi gāthāhi anumoditvā uṭṭhāyāsanā pakkāmi.
root_text.ud8.6:26.1: Tena kho pana samayena sunidhavassakārā magadhamahāmattā bhagavantaṁ piṭṭhito piṭṭhito anubandhā honti:
root_text.ud8.6:26.2: “yenajja samaṇo gotamo dvārena nikkhamissati taṁ gotamadvāraṁ nāma bhavissati.
root_text.ud8.6:26.3: Yena titthena gaṅgaṁ nadiṁ tarissati taṁ gotamatitthaṁ nāma bhavissatī”ti.
root_text.ud8.6:27.1: Atha kho bhagavā yena dvārena nikkhami taṁ gotamadvāraṁ nāma ahosi.
root_text.ud8.6:27.2: Atha kho bhagavā yena gaṅgā nadī tenupasaṅkami.
root_text.ud8.6:27.3: Tena kho pana samayena gaṅgā nadī pūrā hoti samatittikā kākapeyyā.
root_text.ud8.6:27.4: Appekacce manussā nāvaṁ pariyesanti, appekacce uḷumpaṁ pariyesanti, appekacce kullaṁ bandhanti apārā pāraṁ gantukāmā.
root_text.ud8.6:27.5: Atha kho bhagavā—seyyathāpi nāma balavā puriso samiñjitaṁ vā bāhaṁ pasāreyya, pasāritaṁ vā bāhaṁ samiñjeyya; evamevaṁ—gaṅgāya nadiyā orimatīre antarahito pārimatīre paccuṭṭhāsi saddhiṁ bhikkhusaṅghena.
root_text.ud8.6:28.1: Addasā kho bhagavā te manusse appekacce nāvaṁ pariyesante, appekacce uḷumpaṁ pariyesante, appekacce kullaṁ bandhante apārā pāraṁ gantukāme.
root_text.ud8.6:29.1: Atha kho bhagavā etamatthaṁ viditvā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:

translation_text.ud8.6:1.2: At one time the Buddha was wandering in the land of the Magadhans together with a large Saṅgha of mendicants when he arrived at the village of Pāṭali.
translation_text.ud8.6:1.3: The lay followers of Pāṭali Village heard that he had arrived.
translation_text.ud8.6:1.5: So they went to see him, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him,
translation_text.ud8.6:1.7: “Sir, please consent to come to our guest house.”
translation_text.ud8.6:2.1: Then, knowing that the Buddha had consented, the lay followers of Pāṭali Village got up from their seat, bowed, and respectfully circled the Buddha, keeping him on their right. Then they went to the guest house, where they spread carpets all over, prepared seats, set up a water jar, and placed an oil lamp. Then they went back to the Buddha, bowed, stood to one side, and told him of their preparations, saying:
translation_text.ud8.6:3.1: In the morning, the Buddha robed up and, taking his bowl and robe, went to the guest house together with the Saṅgha of mendicants. Having washed his feet he entered the guest house and sat against the central column facing east.
translation_text.ud8.6:3.2: The Saṅgha of mendicants also washed their feet, entered the guest house, and sat against the west wall facing east, with the Buddha right in front of them.
translation_text.ud8.6:3.3: The lay followers of Pāṭali Village also washed their feet, entered the guest house, and sat against the east wall facing west, with the Buddha right in front of them.
translation_text.ud8.6:4.1: “Householders, there are these five drawbacks for an unethical person because of their failure in ethics.
translation_text.ud8.6:4.3: Firstly, an unethical person loses great wealth on account of negligence.
translation_text.ud8.6:5.1: Furthermore, an unethical person gets a bad reputation.
translation_text.ud8.6:6.1: Furthermore, an unethical person enters any kind of assembly timid and embarrassed, whether it’s an assembly of aristocrats, brahmins, householders, or ascetics.
translation_text.ud8.6:7.1: Furthermore, an unethical person feels lost when they die.
translation_text.ud8.6:8.1: Furthermore, an unethical person, when their body breaks up, after death, is reborn in a place of loss, a bad place, the underworld, hell.
translation_text.ud8.6:8.3: These are the five drawbacks for an unethical person because of their failure in ethics.
translation_text.ud8.6:9.1: There are these five benefits for an ethical person because of their accomplishment in ethics.
translation_text.ud8.6:9.3: Firstly, an ethical person gains great wealth on account of diligence.
translation_text.ud8.6:10.1: Furthermore, an ethical person gets a good reputation.
translation_text.ud8.6:11.1: Furthermore, an ethical person enters any kind of assembly bold and self-assured, whether it’s an assembly of aristocrats, brahmins, householders, or ascetics.
translation_text.ud8.6:12.1: Furthermore, an ethical person dies not feeling lost.
translation_text.ud8.6:13.1: Furthermore, when an ethical person’s body breaks up, after death, they’re reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm.
translation_text.ud8.6:13.3: These are the five benefits for an ethical person because of their accomplishment in ethics.”
translation_text.ud8.6:14.1: The Buddha spent much of the night educating, encouraging, firing up, and inspiring the lay followers of Pāṭali Village with a Dhamma talk. Then he sent them off,
translation_text.ud8.6:14.2: “The night is getting late, householders. Please go at your convenience.”
translation_text.ud8.6:14.3: And then the lay followers of Pāṭali Village approved and agreed with what the Buddha said. They got up from their seat, bowed, and respectfully circled the Buddha, keeping him on their right, before leaving.
translation_text.ud8.6:14.4: Soon after they left the Buddha entered a private cubicle.
translation_text.ud8.6:15.1: Now at that time the Magadhan ministers Sunidha and Vassakāra were building a citadel at Pāṭali Village to keep the Vajjis out.
translation_text.ud8.6:15.2: At that time thousands of deities were taking possession of building sites in Pāṭali Village.
translation_text.ud8.6:15.3: Illustrious rulers or royal chief ministers inclined to build houses at sites possessed by illustrious deities.
translation_text.ud8.6:15.4: Middling rulers or royal chief ministers inclined to build houses at sites possessed by middling deities.
translation_text.ud8.6:15.5: Lesser rulers or royal chief ministers inclined to build houses at sites possessed by lesser deities.
translation_text.ud8.6:16.1: With clairvoyance that is purified and superhuman, the Buddha saw those deities taking possession of building sites in Pāṭali Village,
translation_text.ud8.6:16.2: and the people building houses in accord with the station of the deities.
translation_text.ud8.6:16.5: The Buddha rose at the crack of dawn and addressed Ānanda,
translation_text.ud8.6:17.1: “Ānanda, who is building a citadel at Pāṭali Village?”
translation_text.ud8.6:17.2: “Sir, the Magadhan chief ministers Sunidha and Vassakāra are building a citadel to keep the Vajjis out.”
translation_text.ud8.6:17.3: “It’s as if they were building the citadel in consultation with the gods of the thirty-three.
translation_text.ud8.6:17.5: With clairvoyance that is purified and superhuman, I saw those deities taking possession of building sites.
translation_text.ud8.6:17.6: Illustrious rulers or royal chief ministers inclined to build houses at sites possessed by illustrious deities.
translation_text.ud8.6:17.7: Middling rulers or royal chief ministers inclined to build houses at sites possessed by middling deities.
translation_text.ud8.6:17.8: Lesser rulers or royal chief ministers inclined to build houses at sites possessed by lesser deities.
translation_text.ud8.6:17.9: As far as the civilized region extends, as far as the trading zone extends, this will be the chief city: the Pāṭaliputta trade center.
translation_text.ud8.6:17.10: But Pāṭaliputta will face three threats:
translation_text.ud8.6:18.1: Then the Magadhan ministers Sunidha and Vassakāra approached the Buddha, and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.ud8.6:18.2: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, they stood to one side and said,
translation_text.ud8.6:18.4: “Would Mister Gotama together with the mendicant Saṅgha please accept today’s meal from me?”
translation_text.ud8.6:19.1: Then, knowing that the Buddha had consented, they went to their own guest house, where they had delicious fresh and cooked foods prepared. Then they had the Buddha informed of the time, saying,
translation_text.ud8.6:19.2: “It’s time, worthy Gotama, the meal is ready.”
translation_text.ud8.6:20.1: Then the Buddha robed up in the morning and, taking his bowl and robe, went to their guest house together with the mendicant Saṅgha, where he sat on the seat spread out.
translation_text.ud8.6:20.2: Then Sunidha and Vassakāra served and satisfied the mendicant Saṅgha headed by the Buddha with their own hands with delicious fresh and cooked foods.
translation_text.ud8.6:21.1: When the Buddha had eaten and washed his hand and bowl, Sunidha and Vassakāra took a low seat and sat to one side.
translation_text.ud8.6:21.2: The Buddha expressed his appreciation with these verses:
translation_text.ud8.6:22.1: “In the place he makes his dwelling,
translation_text.ud8.6:24.1: After that they have sympathy for him,
translation_text.ud8.6:24.2: like a mother for the child at her breast.
translation_text.ud8.6:24.3: A man beloved of the deities
translation_text.ud8.6:25.1: When the Buddha had expressed his appreciation to Sunidha and Vassakāra with these verses, he got up from his seat and left.
translation_text.ud8.6:26.1: Sunidha and Vassakāra followed behind the Buddha, thinking,
translation_text.ud8.6:26.2: “The gate through which the ascetic Gotama exits today shall be named the Gotama Gate.
translation_text.ud8.6:26.3: The ford at which he crosses the Ganges River shall be named the Gotama Ford.”
translation_text.ud8.6:27.1: Then the gate through which the Buddha exited was named the Gotama Gate.
translation_text.ud8.6:27.2: Then the Buddha came to the Ganges River.
translation_text.ud8.6:27.3: Now at that time the Ganges was full to the brim so a crow could drink from it.
translation_text.ud8.6:27.4: Wanting to cross from the near to the far shore, some people were seeking a boat, some a dinghy, while some were tying up a raft.
translation_text.ud8.6:27.5: But, as easily as a strong person would extend or contract their arm, the Buddha, together with the mendicant Saṅgha, vanished from the near shore and landed on the far shore.
translation_text.ud8.6:28.1: He saw all those humans wanting to cross over.
translation_text.ud8.6:29.1: Then, understanding this matter, on that occasion the Buddha expressed this heartfelt sentiment:
translation_text.ud8.6:30.1: “Those who cross a deluge or stream
translation_text.ud8.6:30.2: have built a bridge and left the marshes behind.
translation_text.ud8.6:30.3: While some people are still tying a raft,

variant_text.ud8.6:1.3: pāṭaligāmiyā → pāṭaligāmikā (dn16:56 [Mahāparinibbānasutta/5. Dussīlaādīnava]) | kho → ko nu kho (sabbattha) | māpentī”ti → māpetīti (sabbattha)
variant_text.ud8.6:2.2: sabbasantharisanthataṁ → sabbasanthariṁ santhataṁ (sya-all, pts-vp-pli1) | udakamaṇiko patiṭṭhāpito → udakamaṇikaṁ patiṭṭhāpitaṁ (sya-all)
